```python
import pytest

def title_case(s: str) -> str:
    return ' '.join(word.capitalize() for word in s.split())

# Happy path tests
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected", [
    ("hello world", "Hello World"),
    ("a b c", "A B C"),
    ("python programming", "Python Programming"),
    ("single", "Single"),
])
def test_title_case_happy_path(input_str, expected):
    assert title_case(input_str) == expected

# Edge case tests
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected", [
    ("", ""),  # Empty string
    ("hello", "Hello"),  # Single word
    ("hello   world", "Hello World"),  # Multiple spaces (split() normalizes to single space)
    ("  hello  ", "Hello"),  # Leading and trailing spaces
    ("hello, world!", "Hello, World!"),  # Punctuation (capitalize doesn't change it)
    ("HELLO WORLD", "Hello World"),  # All uppercase
    ("hElLo WoRlD", "Hello World"),  # Mixed case
    ("123 abc", "123 Abc"),  # Numbers and letters
    ("a", "A"),  # Single character
    ("word-with-hyphen", "Word-With-Hyphen"),  # Hyphenated words
])
def test_title_case_edge_cases(input_str, expected):
    assert title_case(input_str) == expected

# Error case tests (non-string inputs)
@pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_input", [
    123,  # Integer
    None,  # None
    ["hello", "world"],  # List
    {"key": "value"},  # Dict
])
def test_title_case_error_cases(invalid_input):
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        title_case(invalid_input)  # split() not available on non-strings
```
